meta.name: Baked Fish with Garlic, Olive Oil, Lemon, and Herbs

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalog
notes: Mediterranean baseline: olive oil + garlic + herbs + lemon finish.
Designed for adaptable white fish fillets (cod/tilapia/haddock) with safe internal temp guidance.

ingredients.label: White fish fillets (cod, tilapia, haddock), patted dry
ingredients.notes: Target ~4 fillets. For thicker fillets, increase bake time slightly.

ingredients.label: Extra-virgin olive oil
ingredients.notes: Plus extra to grease the pan if needed.

ingredients.label: Lemon (zest + juice)
ingredients.notes: Use zest in the oil mixture; juice for baking and finishing.

ingredients.label: Fine salt
ingredients.notes: Adjust to fish thickness and sides.

steps.title: Preheat oven and prep baking dish
steps.body: Heat oven to 400°F (205°C). Lightly oil a baking dish or line with parchment. Pat fish dry to help the seasoning cling and prevent steaming.

steps.title: Mix garlic-herb olive oil
steps.body: In a bowl, mix olive oil, minced garlic, lemon zest, oregano (optional), a pinch of salt, black pepper (optional), and red pepper flakes (optional).

steps.title: Season fish and add lemon
steps.body: Place fish in the baking dish. Sprinkle remaining salt lightly over fillets. Spoon garlic-herb oil over the top (and under edges if possible). Squeeze 1–2 tablespoons lemon juice over the fish. Scatter capers (optional) around and on top.

steps.title: Bake until flaky and just cooked through
steps.body: Bake 10–14 minutes depending on thickness, until the fish flakes easily and is opaque. If using an instant-read thermometer, target 145°F (63°C) at the thickest part.

steps.title: Finish with herbs and lemon; serve
steps.body: Remove from oven. Sprinkle chopped parsley and/or dill over the fish. Add a final squeeze of lemon to brighten. Spoon pan juices over fillets when serving.

refrigerate.notes: Reheat gently (low oven or covered skillet) to avoid drying. Great cold over salad with extra lemon + olive oil.

variations.title: Foil packet method (extra moist)
changes: Place fish on foil, add garlic-herb oil + lemon, seal packets.
Bake 12–16 minutes (thicker pieces may need longer).
Open carefully to avoid steam burns.

variations.title: Tomato + olive tray bake
changes: Add 250 g cherry tomatoes and 60 g olives to the dish.
Roast tomatoes 8 minutes first, then add fish and bake 10–12 minutes.

variations.title: Butter finish (not strictly core Mediterranean)
changes: Stir 15 g butter into the hot pan juices off-heat for a glossy sauce.
Keep lemon finish to maintain brightness.

notes: Cooking time depends mostly on thickness. Avoid overbaking—fish dries fast.
If your fish is very thin (tilapia), start checking at 8–9 minutes.
Pair with: roasted_vegetables_olive_oil, Greek salad, lemon rice, or pita + hummus.
